_comment: Updated annotation — JBI Case Series checklist for Wakefield et al. 1998 (RETRACTED)

article.title: Ileal-lymphoid-nodular hyperplasia, non-specific colitis, and pervasive developmental disorder in children

study_type.notes: Case series of 12 consecutively referred children with enterocolitis and developmental disorder. Retracted in 2010 due to ethical concerns and methodological flaws.

1.notes: Clear inclusion criteria: children with chronic enterocolitis and regressive developmental disorder; 'consecutively referred children' with normal development followed by loss of acquired skills

2.notes: Standardized condition measurement: ileocolonoscopy and biopsy for GI assessment; neurological and developmental assessment conducted; MRI, EEG performed; standardized histological examination

3.notes: Valid diagnostic methods: ileocolonoscopy (endoscopic gold standard for GI pathology); histopathology for tissue diagnosis; developmental assessment using recognized criteria; radiological imaging (MRI)

4.notes: Consecutive inclusion: 'consecutive series of children...consecutively referred'; sequential enrollment of qualifying patients from pediatric gastroenterology unit

5.notes: Complete inclusion: all 12 consecutively referred children included; no post-hoc exclusions reported; complete sample of qualifying referrals during study period

6.notes: Demographics reported: mean age 6 years (range 3-10); 11 boys, 1 girl; gender and age distribution clearly specified in summary

7.notes: Clinical information: medical history with normal development followed by regression; loss of acquired skills including language; diarrhea and abdominal pain documented; prior illness histories

8.notes: Outcomes clearly reported: histological findings (lymphoid nodular hyperplasia, chronic inflammation, aphthoid ulceration); neurological abnormalities (autism in 9, psychosis in 1); behavioral disorders documented

9.notes: Site demographics: Royal Free Hospital, London; pediatric gastroenterology unit; tertiary referral center setting specified

10.notes: Statistical analysis: descriptive analysis appropriate; histopathological frequencies reported (11/12 chronic colitis, 7/12 lymphoid hyperplasia); Mann-Whitney and chi-square tests used
